system.description:
CLASS DICE: D6

Foolhardy

You can add your D6 to your dice pool any time you're acting in a foolish, daring or cavalier fashion. In any dice pool which includes the Fool's D6, you gain:

Special: Roll another D6 and add it to the present dice pool.

Always look on the bright side of life, as the song goes. It's true.

If a Fool's laughing, you'll get out of this hell alive. If you get a laugh, your enemies won't be laughing for much longer. Don't take things too seriously, and it won't get too serious. At least, for the Fool.

The Fool's the swashbuckler, a romantic rogue, the life and soul of the adventuring party.

Fools rush in... and their friends have to deal with the consequences.

Play a Fool if you want to...

  • Bound into action with a grin, knowing your luck will protect you.
  • Get out of any situation by the skin of your teeth, even if it means making a few sacrifices.
  • Deal with the chaos when pushing your powers too far inevitably bites everyone on the ass.
system.coreNature:
Always look on the bright side of life, as the song goes. It's true.

If a Fool's laughing, you'll get out of this hell alive. If you get a laugh, your enemies won't be laughing for much longer. Don't take things too seriously, and it won't get too serious. At least, for the Fool.

The Fool's the swashbuckler, a romantic rogue, the life and soul of the adventuring party. The rest think they're irresponsible, but the Fool's abilities mean the only responsible thing to do is be irresponsible.

Screw everyone else if they can't take a joke.

Fools rush in... and their friends have to deal with the consequences.

PLAY A FOOL IF YOU WANT TO...

  • Bound into action with a grin, knowing your luck will protect you.
  • Get out of any situation by the skin of your teeth, even if it means making a few sacrifices.
  • Deal with the chaos when pushing your powers too far inevitably bites everyone on the ass.

coreAbility.name: FOOLHARDY
coreAbility.description:
You can add your D6 to your dice pool any time you're acting in a foolish, daring or cavalier fashion. In any dice pool which includes the Fool's D6, you gain:

Special: Roll another D6 and add it to the present dice pool.

looks.name: Light fabrics, exposed skin, a big grin
Black leather, a deck of cards and fast fingers
A long cloak balanced between elegantly-wasted and scruffy

specials.name: Roll another D6
specials.description: Add it to the present dice pool

fields.label: Your Trade
fields.description: Choose your trade. This determines additional equipment and abilities.
choices: Swashbuckler - Extra equipment option
Trickster Wizard - Cast minor spells (Cha pool)
Rogue - Throwing weapons & thieves' tools
Bard - Musical instrument & songbook
Con Artist - Fake dice, marked cards, etc.
Hustler - Bag of questionable trading wares

fields.label: Flukes
fields.initial:
"

Choose one face of your D6 and mark it with a circle. When you roll your D6:

  • Circle: Describe a fluke of luck that benefits you. GM may modify it. Then erase all but one circle, and add a cross to a different face.
  • Unmarked: Add a circle to a different face (unless all sides are full).
  • Cross: Something unfortunate happens to you or those around you. Erase all crosses.

If you do something particularly foolish or entertaining, the GM may give you an additional circle.

"

fields.label: D6 Face Tracking
fields.description: Track circles (○) and crosses (×) on each face of your D6.

fields.label: If All Else Fails
fields.initial:
"

If you're in a dire situation, hand the GM your D6. The GM will describe the unfeasibly good luck that rescues you (and possibly your friends) from danger.

While the GM has your D6, they can describe an entirely unfair event on a similar or lower scale of awfulness and then give you back your D6. If you deliberately cause a spectacular or amusing disaster, take your D6 back.

"

fields.label: A Trip to Clown School
fields.description: Pick tricks you've learned to use (and abuse) your luck:
choices: LOVE FOOL - Auto-succeed for love (1/session)
FOOLS RUSH IN - Lose all Guard for first action
FOOL'S PREP - Declare needed item, add cross to get it
OH, IGNORE THAT FOOL - Add cross to be underestimated
FOOL'S GOLD - Add cross to know what someone wants
NEVER TELL ME THE ODDS - Never lose games of chance
FOOL'S CHARM - Add cross for instant trust
LUCKY RABBIT'S FOOT - Foolhardy act adds circle

fields.label: Knacks & Spells
fields.description: Each time you take this advancement, choose a Knack OR a Spell:
fields: {"key":"knacks","type":"html","label":"Knacks","initial":"","hint":"Things you're good at - get Advantage when doing them.","description":""}
{"key":"spells","type":"itemList","label":"Spells Known","initial":"[]","hint":"Spells from Grimoire (max 15 risk). Cast with Cha pool.","description":"","itemType":"spell"}

fields.label: Clean Slate
fields.description: Between scenarios you may delete all circles and crosses from the dice, and add a single circle. When you do so, describe what your Fool does to blow off steam.

fields.label: Jack of All Trades
fields.description: Select a second Trade and gain all bonuses/equipment from it:
choices: Swashbuckler
Trickster Wizard
Rogue
Bard
Con Artist
Hustler

fields.label: It's A Stupid Plan... But It Just Might Work
fields.description: 1/session, add a cross to automatically pass a roll with minimum successes. The plan must be highly unlikely and foolish. If it doesn't make the table think "That'll never work," it isn't foolish enough.

fields.label: Pushing It
fields.description: Like Stupid Plan, describe a foolish plan and add a cross, then roll. If you don't roll a cross, the plan succeeds. If you roll a cross, the plan goes desperately wrong - GM describes how (could include serious loss or death of friendly characters).

fields.label: The Gig
fields.description: If you have a captive audience and time to prepare and perform a dramatic ritual, set your D6 to 6 on your performance roll.

fields.label: King for a Day
fields.description: Take over a whole Great Power (Empire, kingdom, guild) for the current adventure.
fields: {"key":"kingdomTaken","type":"html","label":"How did you take power?","initial":"","hint":"First use is free. Additional uses require adding a permanent cross.","description":""}

fields.label: Clownshow
fields.description: 1/session, say "This is turning into a total clownshow". For the rest of the encounter, any time anyone fails a roll, an unfortunate event happens to them (or those near them). Friends, enemies, everyone. The Fool is not immune.

fields.label: Holy Fool
fields.description: Whenever you act with kindness and altruism, you can add your D6 to the pool. If you ever take Don't Give A Fuck, lose this ability.

fields.label: Don't Give a Fuck
fields.description: You're never directly affected negatively by any unfortunate fallout of your abilities (Flukes, If All Else Fails, Pushing It). Any bad luck will only ever hurt people around you. If you ever take Holy Fool, lose this ability.

fields.label: Lucky Break (Level 6)
fields.description: The GM offers something you want/need with a consequence if you agree.
fields: {"key":"luckyBreakDescription","type":"html","label":"What happened?","initial":"","hint":"Circle = free. Cross = consequence only. Other = both.","description":""}
